name: La Mongie Climb Sector
roadSegment: D918 La Mongie, about 4.6 km before Col du Tourmalet summit on Stage 6
summary: Potentially more serviceable Tourmalet-side option than the summit, with riders still climbing hard and La Mongie nearby.
whyItMatters: La Mongie is an official schedule point before the Tourmalet summit, and town/resort infrastructure may make a long mountain wait more manageable if access is allowed.

access.estimated_route_from_base: From Arreau in normal conditions, road access would involve the Col d'Aspin/Sainte-Marie-de-Campan side before climbing to La Mongie; race-day feasibility unknown.
access.parking_transit_possibilities: Unknown. La Mongie has resort infrastructure in normal conditions, but no official Tour parking/shuttle plan found.
access.walk_bike_approach_notes: Could be a walk/bike target from lower closures if permitted; exact distances and permissions unknown.

pros: Official time-schedule point with riders still on the HC Tourmalet climb.
May have more food/toilet/shelter options than the bare summit if businesses are open and accessible.
Could reduce summit crowd crush while preserving mountain-stage atmosphere.
cons: Still dependent on mountain-road closure and parking rules.
Resort roads can become bottlenecks and may be reserved for race/official vehicles.
Less symbolic than the summit and likely still crowded.

logistics.parking: Unknown; verify official La Mongie parking or shuttle before selecting.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Needs official closure order for D918 and La Mongie access.

crowdRisk: high: major climb and official route point, but possibly less compressed than the summit.
bathroomFoodProximity: Potentially better than summit because La Mongie is a resort area; actual open/reachable facilities on race day are unknown.
escapeRoute: Expect controlled, slow descent after the race; likely back down toward Sainte-Marie-de-Campan/Payolle only when authorities reopen roads.
safetyAssessment: Choose a broad, off-road/resort-side area behind barriers; avoid avalanche-gallery edges, blind bends, and convoy pinch points.

timing.best_arrival_window: Morning or earlier, adjusted to official closure time.
timing.race_day_risk: High until access, parking, and facility availability are confirmed.

sources.title: Tour de France 2026 Stage 6 official page
sources.note: Supports La Mongie as an official schedule point before Col du Tourmalet and provides rider/caravan timing estimates.

sources.title: La Mongie background
sources.note: Supports La Mongie resort context, altitude, Tourmalet proximity, and normal access background; not a race-day logistics source.

sources.title: Col du Tourmalet background
sources.note: Supports the D918 climb/pass context and mountain-road access caveats.

open_questions: Will La Mongie be open to general spectators, and from which side?
Are resort toilets/restaurants open and outside the race perimeter?
What is the official parking/shuttle/bike-up policy for the Tourmalet stage?
